skill.name: traffic-analysis
skill.description: Analyze network traffic patterns, DPI categories, bandwidth usage, and device throughput to support traffic management decisions.
skill.summary: Analyze network traffic usage, DPI categories, and bandwidth bottlenecks.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported nine external-command alerts and three weak-cryptography alerts. Review found the command alerts are Markdown inline code spans for tool names, and the cryptography alerts are substring matches in ordinary prose, so no confirmed security issue was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool Names Parsed as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged lines 33, 34, 35, 36, 41, 47, 54, 55, and 56 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines only contain Markdown inline code spans naming available tools and workflow steps, with no executable script or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is Markdown documentation using inline code formatting around tool names. No shell, Ruby, script file, or command execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged lines 3, 27, and 84 as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. These lines contain prose such as the frontmatter description and bandwidth upgrade guidance, with no cryptographic operation or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic code, keys, hashes, ciphers, or security protocol configuration. The matches are ordinary English text in documentation.

content.user_title: Analyze Network Traffic and Bandwidth Usage
content.value_statement: Network teams need clear views of bandwidth use, application categories, and device throughput. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through traffic analysis workflows using available network tools.
actual_capabilities: Lists DPI application categories in use on the network.
Reviews client bandwidth and traffic statistics.
Checks device-level traffic and throughput metrics.
Identifies top bandwidth consumers and heavy application categories.
Suggests traffic management and QoS priorities from observed usage.
Structures traffic reports with congestion and optimization notes.
limitations: Requires connected network tools that expose DPI, client, and device statistics.
Does not capture packets or inspect raw traffic payloads.
Recommendations depend on the accuracy and freshness of available metrics.
Does not enforce QoS policies or change network configuration by itself.

use_cases.title: Investigate Congestion
use_cases.description: Find clients, devices, and application categories that are driving high bandwidth use during slow network periods.

use_cases.title: Plan Bandwidth Capacity
use_cases.description: Review traffic trends and heavy-use categories to support upgrade decisions and capacity planning.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Business Traffic
use_cases.description: Use DPI categories and throughput data to design fair QoS priorities for important applications.

prompt_templates.title: Show Top Traffic Categories
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze current DPI categories and summarize which application groups are using the most bandwidth.

prompt_templates.title: Find Heavy Clients
prompt_templates.prompt: Review client traffic statistics and identify the top bandwidth consumers with likely causes.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Slow Network
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare device throughput, client bandwidth, and DPI categories to identify possible congestion sources.

prompt_templates.title: Build QoS Recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze traffic distribution and propose QoS priorities for business, streaming, gaming, and background traffic.

output_examples.input: What applications are using the most bandwidth?
output_examples.output: A ranked summary of DPI categories, estimated traffic share, likely business impact, and suggested follow-up checks.

output_examples.input: Identify bandwidth bottlenecks for the last reporting period.
output_examples.output: A concise report covering top clients, high-throughput devices, congestion signals, and practical optimization options.

output_examples.input: Recommend QoS policies based on current traffic.
output_examples.output: A policy-focused summary that prioritizes business traffic and notes categories that may need rate limits or monitoring.

best_practices: Compare DPI categories with client and device statistics before drawing conclusions.
Review traffic during normal and peak periods to avoid misleading recommendations.
Document policy changes and recheck traffic trends after each adjustment.
anti_patterns: Do not treat one short traffic sample as a long-term trend.
Do not prioritize applications without considering business requirements.
Do not assume DPI categories explain every network performance issue.

faq.question: Does this skill collect packet captures?
faq.answer: No. It works with available DPI, client, device, and throughput statistics.

faq.question: Can it change network settings?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend traffic policies, but it does not apply configuration changes.

faq.question: Which tools does it reference?
faq.answer: It references tools for DPI categories, client statistics, network devices, and device throughput metrics.

faq.question: Is it useful without DPI data?
faq.answer: It can still review client and device usage, but application classification will be limited.

faq.question: Can it help justify bandwidth upgrades?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize traffic evidence and identify growth patterns that support upgrade planning.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the time period, affected network area, and any known performance symptoms.
